intent: We are not building the Forge Agent architecture. We are building a Mac Consensus Dev Agent that will use consensus AI to develop high quality software. Use all the documents in the local repo to determine the architecture.
scope_statement:
Build the complete Consensus Dev Agent — a native macOS AI coding agent with a two-process architecture (Swift shell + Python backend) that uses dual-LLM consensus generation (Claude + GPT-4o with Claude arbitrating) to autonomously decompose build intents into ordered PRDs and PRs, generate implementation code and tests through an 8-stage build pipeline with 3-pass review, execute CI validation, and gate on operator approval before merging. The system encompasses 16 TRDs covering the macOS application shell (TRD-1), consensus engine (TRD-2), build pipeline (TRD-3), multi-agent coordination (TRD-4), GitHub integration (TRD-5), holistic code review (TRD-6), TRD development workflow with adaptive FOUNDER/ENGINEER/CONSULTANT modes (TRD-7), UI/UX design system with Figma pipeline (TRD-8), Mac CI runner infrastructure (TRD-9), document store with FAISS vector retrieval (TRD-10), security threat model (TRD-11), backend runtime startup (TRD-12), recovery and state management (TRD-13), code quality CI pipeline (TRD-14), operational runbook (TRD-15), and agent testing/validation (TRD-16).

Clarifications:
  - Forge should be the user facing name with Dev Agent as a subheader
  - Review all the TRDs and if there is any ambiguity then resolve them in the PRD creation.
  - Review all the TRDs and if there is any ambiguity then resolve them in the PRD creation.
  - Review all the TRDs and if there is any ambiguity then resolve them in the PRD creation. Use best practices
  - Review all the TRDs and if there is any ambiguity then resolve them in the PRD creation. Use best practices

prd_plan.title: Product Foundation, Repository Bootstrap, and Cross-TRD Contract Baseline
Swift macOS Application Shell, Lifecycle, and Root Navigation
Authentication, Session Security, Keychain, and Secret Delivery
Authenticated Swift–Python IPC Channel and Two-Process Runtime Handshake
Python Backend Runtime Startup, Health Model, and Degraded Operation
Build Stream UI, Card System, Gates, and Operator Interaction Model
Document Import, Parsing UX, and Project Document Management
Document Store, Parsing Engine, Embeddings, FAISS Retrieval, and Injection Defenses
Consensus Engine, Provider Adapters, Arbitration, and Native Output Enforcement
GitHub Integration Layer, Repository Bootstrap, and Branch/PR Lifecycle
Multi-Agent Build Ledger, Coordination Protocol, and Shared Build Visibility
Directed Build Pipeline Orchestration and Stage Contract Implementation
PRD Planning, PRD Generation, and Ordered PR Planning Engine
Code Generation, Sanitization, Native Output Gate, and Autonomous Fix Loop
Three-Pass Review, Security Review, and Holistic Review Workflow
CI Validation Gates, Workflow Generation, and Ubuntu/macOS Routing
Recovery, Thread State Store, Resume Logic, and Mid-PR Checkpointing
TRD Development Workflow with Adaptive FOUNDER/ENGINEER/CONSULTANT Modes
Design Ingestion UX, Sketch Interpretation, and Figma Pipeline
Security Threat Model Enforcement, Audit Logging, and Safe Failure Defaults
Mac Self-Hosted CI Runner, Signing, Notarization, and Swift Validation Infrastructure
Code Quality CI Pipeline and Development Workflow Tooling
Operational Runbook, Diagnostics, and Production Operator Workflows
Comprehensive Testing, Validation, Coverage, and Release Readiness
Release Packaging, Sparkle Updates, Distribution, and Final Product Hardening

1.title: Create canonical VERSION file and versioning contract specification
1.summary: Create the single source-of-truth VERSION file at the repository root containing the initial semantic version (0.1.0). Include docs/VERSIONING.md defining the semantic versioning contract: how VERSION relates to CFBundleShortVersionString in Info.plist, AGENT_VERSION in Python's version.py, version in pyproject.toml, and the version handshake protocol between Swift shell and Python backend. Define version bump rules (major = breaking IPC/contract change, minor = new capability, patch = bugfix), pre-release tag format, and the CI enforcement rule that all representations must match at build time.

2.title: Establish cross-TRD conflict resolution hierarchy and ambiguity resolutions
2.summary: Create docs/CONFLICT_RESOLUTION.md defining the complete TRD precedence hierarchy: TRD-11 (security) overrides all TRDs on security matters → owning TRD governs its own domain → standards docs (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md) break ties → best-practice defaults. Document all specific ambiguity resolutions across the 16 TRDs: startup timeout values (TRD-1 vs TRD-12), overlapping code quality gates (TRD-3 vs TRD-14), configuration key naming conflicts, error taxonomy overlaps, and logging level definitions. Each resolution includes the conflicting sources, the chosen resolution, and the rationale. Also create forge-standards/decision-precedence.md as a quick-reference lookup.

3.title: Define product identity, naming conventions, and branding rules
3.summary: Create docs/PRODUCT_IDENTITY.md defining: 'Forge' as user-facing product name, 'Dev Agent' as subheader, usage rules for code vs UI vs docs, and prohibited forms. Create docs/NAMING_CONVENTIONS.md with comprehensive rules: branch naming (feature/<prd>-<pr>-<slug>, fix/<issue>-<slug>, release/<semver>), file naming (snake_case Python, PascalCase Swift types, camelCase Swift variables), directory naming (lowercase-hyphenated for docs/config, snake_case Python packages, PascalCase Swift groups), artifact naming (<forge>-<component>-<version>-<arch>.<ext>), state/enum naming (UPPER_SNAKE constants, PascalCase enums), configuration key naming (dot-separated hierarchical: forge.backend.consensus.timeout), and repository layout specification.

4.title: Bootstrap complete repository directory skeleton
4.summary: Create the canonical directory structure for the entire project. Swift shell: ForgeApp/ with Sources/ForgeApp/ (App/, IPC/, UI/, Services/), ForgeAgent/, ForgeAgentTests/, Resources/. Python backend: src/forge/ package (consensus/, pipeline/, agents/, github_integration/, review/, document_store/, recovery/, config/, utils/, errors/, contracts/), each with __init__.py. Shared: config/defaults/, docs/ (trds/, prds/, runbooks/, architecture/), forge-standards/, scripts/ (ci/, dev/, setup/), tests/ (unit/, integration/, e2e/, fixtures/), .github/workflows/, contracts/, state/, artifacts/. Include .gitkeep in empty directories. Create .gitignore with Python, Swift, macOS, IDE patterns. Create scripts/validate_structure.py that asserts the directory tree matches the canonical layout for CI enforcement.

5.title: Tests for repository skeleton and naming convention compliance
5.summary: Automated tests validating the bootstrapped repository layout and naming conventions from PRs 1-4. Test directory completeness: verify every required directory exists, every __init__.py is present, no unexpected top-level directories. Test naming conventions: scan all Python files for snake_case module names, verify directory naming patterns. Test validate_structure.py itself: run it and verify it passes on the current repo, test that it detects a missing directory. Test presence of canonical documents (VERSION, VERSIONING.md, CONFLICT_RESOLUTION.md, NAMING_CONVENTIONS.md, PRODUCT_IDENTITY.md).

6.title: Create Python project scaffolding with pyproject.toml and version module
6.summary: Create pyproject.toml with project metadata (name='forge-dev-agent'), build system configuration (hatchling), dependency groups (core, dev, test, lint), Python version constraint (>=3.11), and version sourced dynamically from the VERSION file. Create src/forge/version.py that reads VERSION file and exposes AGENT_VERSION constant, version_tuple(), and is_compatible(remote_version) for the handshake protocol. Create src/forge/py.typed marker. Create requirements/ directory with base.txt, dev.txt, test.txt for pip-based workflows. Ensure version in pyproject.toml matches VERSION file via a check in version.py module load.

7.title: Create Swift project scaffolding with Package.swift and version binding
7.summary: Create the Swift package/Xcode project skeleton: ForgeApp/Package.swift defining the ForgeApp target and ForgeAgentTests test target. Create ForgeApp/Sources/ForgeApp/App/ForgeApp.swift as a minimal SwiftUI app entry point displaying 'Forge' with 'Dev Agent' subheader. Create ForgeApp/Sources/ForgeApp/App/ForgeVersion.swift that reads the root VERSION file at build time and exposes it as a static property. Create ForgeApp/Sources/ForgeApp/Info.plist with CFBundleShortVersionString placeholder. Create ForgeApp/Config/Base.xcconfig, Debug.xcconfig, Release.xcconfig, Version.xcconfig. Create scripts/sync_version.sh that validates VERSION == Info.plist == pyproject.toml == ForgeVersion.swift constant.

8.title: Define shared error taxonomy and error types
8.summary: Create the unified error taxonomy used across both Swift and Python. Python: src/forge/errors/base.py defining the hierarchy: ForgeError (base) with subtypes ForgeConfigError, ForgeIPCError, ForgeConsensusError, ForgePipelineError, ForgeGitHubError, ForgeReviewError, ForgeSecurityError, ForgeRecoveryError, ForgeValidationError. Each includes error_code (string, e.g. 'FORGE-IPC-001'), severity (enum: critical, error, warning), is_retryable (bool), and context dict, plus serialization to dict for IPC transport. Swift: ForgeApp/Sources/ForgeApp/Services/ForgeError.swift with matching error enum. Create docs/architecture/error_taxonomy.md documenting all error codes, meanings, retry policies, and TRD ownership per error domain. Resolve overlapping definitions across TRD-1, TRD-12, TRD-13 per CONFLICT_RESOLUTION.md.

9.title: Define shared configuration model, schema, and defaults
9.summary: Create the unified configuration system. Python: src/forge/config/settings.py with a Pydantic BaseSettings model (ForgeSettings) defining all configuration keys using dot-separated hierarchical naming with typed defaults, environment variable overrides (FORGE_ prefix), and YAML file loading. src/forge/config/loader.py implementing config precedence: environment vars > user config > project config > defaults. Create config/defaults/forge.yaml with all default values. Create config/forge-config.schema.json as machine-readable JSON Schema. Create src/forge/config/capabilities.py for capability flags (consensus_enabled, review_3pass_enabled, ci_validation_enabled). Swift: ForgeApp/Sources/ForgeApp/Services/ForgeSettings.swift matching structure reading from UserDefaults and shared config. Create docs/architecture/configuration-model.md documenting the full model.

10.title: Tests for version module, configuration loader, and error types
10.summary: Comprehensive tests for foundational Python modules from PRs 6, 8, 9. Version tests: AGENT_VERSION matches VERSION file, version_tuple() returns correct tuple, is_compatible() handles major/minor/patch rules. Config tests: ForgeSettings loads defaults from forge.yaml, env overrides with FORGE_ prefix work, config precedence (env > file > defaults), invalid config raises ForgeConfigError, capability flags default correctly, JSON Schema validates defaults. Error tests: hierarchy verification (all errors inherit ForgeError), error_code format validation, severity enum values, is_retryable defaults per type, serialization to dict for IPC, context dict round-trip.

11.title: Define IPC interface contracts and message schemas
11.summary: Create machine-readable interface contracts for Swift-Python IPC. Create docs/architecture/ipc_contract.md documenting: Unix domain socket path, JSON message envelope ({"version", "msg_type", "correlation_id", "payload"}), all message types (ready_handshake, build_intent, pipeline_status, review_result, approval_request, approval_response, error), version compatibility rules. Python: src/forge/contracts/message_types.py (enum), schemas.py (Pydantic models per envelope/payload), handshake.py (version compatibility logic). Swift: ForgeApp/Sources/ForgeApp/IPC/IPCMessageTypes.swift with matching Codable structs. Machine-readable: contracts/forge-ipc-api.json and docs/architecture/ipc_schema.json for cross-language validation. Create contracts/ready-handshake.json specifying startup handshake sequence and compatibility negotiation.

12.title: Tests for IPC contracts, message schemas, and handshake logic
12.summary: Comprehensive tests for IPC contracts from PR-11. Message types: all expected types defined, enum values unique, string representations match contract doc. Schemas: each Pydantic model validates correct payloads, rejects malformed payloads, serializes to JSON matching ipc_schema.json, round-trips through JSON. Handshake: version compatibility logic (same major = compatible, different major = incompatible, newer minor backend = compatible with warning), ready-handshake sequence validation. Schema validation: load ipc_schema.json and validate all example messages. Cross-language consistency: Python message type strings match Swift IPCMessageTypes values from documentation.

13.title: Create AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and architecture context documents
13.summary: Create AI agent context files guiding all subsequent development. AGENTS.md: project context including directory layout reference, version contract reference, naming convention reference, TRD precedence hierarchy reference, coding standards (Python: black, ruff, mypy strict; Swift: SwiftLint, SwiftFormat), commit message format (conventional commits), PR template, list of all 16 TRDs with summaries. CLAUDE.md: AI-specific instructions including conflict resolution hierarchy, security-first principles, TRD-11 override rule, coding constraints. docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md: two-process architecture overview (ASCII diagram), component interaction map, data flow. docs/architecture/system-context.md: broader system context. docs/architecture/source-of-truth-index.md mapping each concern to its canonical file.

14.title: Create foundational CI workflow for structure, version, and lint validation
14.summary: Create .github/workflows/foundation-checks.yml running on every PR and push to main. Jobs: (1) validate-structure: runs scripts/validate_structure.py, (2) validate-version-sync: runs scripts/sync_version.sh ensuring VERSION == pyproject.toml == Info.plist == ForgeVersion.swift, (3) validate-contracts: runs scripts/validate_contracts.py checking JSON schemas are valid and contract files present, (4) python-lint: runs ruff check and mypy on src/forge/, (5) python-test: runs pytest on tests/unit/ with coverage, (6) swift-build: builds ForgeApp to verify compilation. Create .github/pull_request_template.md with checklist. Create .pre-commit-config.yaml with hooks for ruff, black, trailing whitespace, version sync. Create scripts/validate_contracts.py and scripts/check_version_sync.py.

15.title: Create README.md, developer setup, contributing guide, and bootstrap script
15.summary: Create root README.md with Forge branding, one-paragraph description, architecture overview (two-process: Swift shell + Python backend, dual-LLM consensus, 8-stage pipeline), prerequisites (macOS 14+, Xcode 15+, Python 3.11+), quick start guide, links to architecture docs, VERSIONING.md, contributing guidelines. Create scripts/setup/bootstrap.sh: creates Python venv, installs requirements, runs validate_structure.py, runs sync_version.sh, installs pre-commit hooks, validates contracts. Create CONTRIBUTING.md with branch naming rules, PR process, commit message format, references to NAMING_CONVENTIONS.md. Create LICENSE placeholder. Create docs/development/bootstrap-workflow.md documenting the full onboarding flow.

16.title: Document PRD-001 baseline decisions and publish source-of-truth index
16.summary: Create docs/prds/PRD-001.md as the finalized record of all foundational decisions made across the preceding PRs: versioning contract, conflict resolution outcomes, naming conventions adopted, directory layout rationale, IPC contract baseline, error taxonomy, configuration model, and capability flags. Cross-reference every foundational concern to its canonical file via docs/architecture/source-of-truth-index.md (updating it from PR-13 with final file paths). This serves as the onboarding index for all subsequent PRDs and provides traceability from requirements to implementation artifacts.

17.title: Integration tests for full bootstrap and cross-component consistency
17.summary: End-to-end integration tests validating the entire foundation works together. Bootstrap test: run scripts/setup/bootstrap.sh in clean env, verify venv created, deps install, validate_structure.py passes, sync_version.sh passes, validate_contracts.py passes. Version round-trip: import forge.version, verify AGENT_VERSION matches VERSION, create mock IPC handshake via schemas.py and verify version field. Config-error integration: create ForgeSettings with invalid config, verify ForgeConfigError with correct error_code format. Contract alignment: verify Python message types match contract JSON definitions, handshake schema matches ready-handshake.json. Naming convention scan: verify all Python module names are snake_case, class names PascalCase. Directory completeness: verify every __init__.py and .gitkeep exists.
